task_completion.description: Configure Apache to hide OS and version details in the Server header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like nano or vim) or a terminal command (like sed or echo) modifying an Apache configuration file (e.g., /etc/apache2/conf-available/security.conf or apache2.conf) to set 'ServerTokens Prod'.

task_completion.description: Configure Apache to disable the server signature on server-generated pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal command modifying an Apache configuration file to set 'ServerSignature Off'.

task_completion.description: Configure PHP to remove the PHP version header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal command modifying a php.ini file (e.g., /etc/php/7.4/apache2/php.ini) to set 'expose_php = Off'.

task_completion.description: Configure Apache to disable directory browsing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal command modifying an Apache configuration file (e.g., apache2.conf or a site-specific config) to remove 'Indexes' from the 'Options' directive, or explicitly setting 'Options -Indexes'.

task_completion.description: Apply the configuration changes to the Apache service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command executing 'systemctl restart apache2', 'systemctl reload apache2', or 'service apache2 restart' to apply the changes.

task_completion.description: Verify that the headers are hidden and directory browsing is forbidden.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'curl -I' command or browser network tab showing the 'Server: Apache' header (with no version/OS), the absence of the 'X-Powered-By: PHP' header, and a 'curl' or browser request to '/test_indexes/' returning a '403 Forbidden' error.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually edited the configuration files rather than just printing the required text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent used a legitimate text editor (nano, vim) or valid shell commands (sed, echo >>) targeting the actual configuration files in /etc/apache2/ or /etc/php/.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent executed the restart command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the restart command was actually submitted in the terminal (e.g., a new prompt line appears after the command), not just typed without pressing Enter.

integrity.description: Ensure the verification tests are genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the agent tested the configuration, verify that the curl outputs or browser screens look like genuine HTTP responses and not mocked text output via 'echo'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected 'Server' HTTP header must be exactly 'Apache' (no version or OS details). The vulnerable directory to test for a 403 Forbidden error is exactly '/test_indexes/'.
